free_text: Measurements of 10Be and 26Al production cross
sections with 12 GeV protons by accelerator mass
spectrometry
College of Humanities and Sciences, Nihon
University, Tokyo
School of Economics, Otemon Gakuin
University, Ibaragi, Osaka
Beam intensity is 2.62e+11 PPS.
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Backing: Each target foil was guarded on both sides
with identical foils in order to compensate for
recoil losses and to prevent cross-contamination
between the targets.
Irradiation for 10 min
Separated using 200 micro-gram of Be and 500
micro-gram of Al carriers
Isotopic ratios of 10Be/9Be and 26Al/27Al were
determined by the AMS system using an internal
beam-monitor method
For Activation
Used in accelerator mass spectrometry
Uncertainties due to the beam-intensity
calibration not included
Uncertainties due to the beam-intensity
calibration (about 10%)
Uncertainties in the amounts of beryllium and
aluminium carriers added
Universal formula for target mass dependence of
production cross section [X.Campi et al., Phys.
